company_type: Regulated Utility - Natural Gas and Electric

dependencies: Dependence on third-party transmission lines and natural gas pipelines for energy delivery
Reliance on coal and natural gas for electric generation
Long lead times for construction materials and equipment
Dependence on third-party developers for renewable generation projects under Build-Transfer Agreements
risks: Supply chain constraints including material and labor shortages impacting gas and electric projects
Increasing delivery lead times for construction materials
Lack of diversity in suppliers of some gas materials
Delayed deliveries and unavailability of materials
Increased prices for materials, equipment and products due to inflationary pressures

regulatory_risks: Rate regulation by state utility commissions and FERC
Regulatory rate reviews and proceedings with uncertain outcomes
Potential disallowance of operating or capital costs in rate recovery
Downward adjustments in allowed Return on Equity by regulators
NERC Critical Infrastructure Protection standards for electric operations
PHMSA pipeline safety regulations including proposed LDAR rule and SGDP rule
EPA regulations on greenhouse gas emissions for fossil fuel-fired power plants
EPA Coal Combustion Residuals (CCR) regulations including Legacy CCR Rule
Environmental regulations related to air quality, water quality, hazardous waste and solid waste
CERCLA (Superfund) liability at waste disposal sites
Manufactured Gas Plant (MGP) site remediation requirements
Maryland Climate Solutions Now Act requiring 60% GHG reduction by 2031 and net zero by 2045
Maryland policies on electrification and Clean Heat Standard
Potential future carbon pricing regimes or methane fees
Changes in tax laws including IRA provisions
Compliance with changing pipeline safety regulations under PIPES Act of 2020
